rows.notes: Clear printed title at top left. Very legible, no monetary columns on this line.
Clear centered section heading underlined on the page.
Inner currency columns clear. 'cr.' abbreviation visible at left of numbers; numbers read as 5,716 pounds, 4 shillings, 6 pence with good alignment to the inner £ s d columns.
Inner set of £ s d columns clearly shows 236 pounds, 15 shillings, 10 pence for the Library Account (aligned in the inner columns).

Outer set of £ s d columns (far right) shows '5952 . 4' immediately to the right of the Library/Cash lines. Extracted per page-specific guidance. The outer figure alignment is clear, but arithmetic with inner lines appears inconsistent (see note) so confidence reduced slightly.
Inner columns show '3000' with no shillings/pence indicated. Clear, well aligned in inner £ column.
Inner set of £ s d columns clearly reads 6,511 pounds, 14 shillings, 2 pence; alignment to inner column set is clear.

Outer set of £ s d columns (far right) shows '9571 14 2' aligned as a second set of amounts for Investments. The outer columns are fainter but the digits are well-formed and align with the outer total column; extracted per page-specific guidance.
Underlined section heading introducing several income lines; clearly legible and centered left of amounts.
Inner £ column shows '134' with no shillings/pence; alignment clear in inner columns.

Description read as 'Tithes' (handwritten - the first letter slightly ambiguous but context of parish accounts supports 'Tithes'). Amount in inner columns reads clearly as 224 pounds, 15 shillings, 3 pence.
Inner £ s d columns clearly aligned to 53 pounds, 13 shillings, 4 pence.
Inner columns show '1 15' (1 pound, 15 shillings). Pence column blank; alignment to inner columns clear.
Inner columns show '70' clearly. This line sits directly above the group subtotal in the outer columns.

Group subtotal for the 'Due to the College' block, taken from the outer £ s d columns per page-specific guidance. Numerals well aligned in the outer columns and clearly read as 484 pounds, 3 shillings, 7 pence.
Handwritten description slightly stylized but readable as 'How Exhibition Fund'; inner columns show 18 pounds, 10 shillings, 11 pence aligned to inner set.
Name 'Michell' is legible in handwriting. Inner columns show 16 pounds, 14 shillings; pence column blank. Alignment to inner set clear.
Handwritten fund name read as 'Lorcombe Richards Fund' (final letters slightly ornate but readable). Inner columns show 19 pounds, 12 shillings, 7 pence; alignment to inner set clear.
Subtotal for the small funds block taken from the outer £ s d columns as indicated by page-specific guidance. Outer numerals align cleanly and read 54 pounds, 17 shillings, 6 pence.

rows.description: I certify that the Accounts of the College are duly kept in proper books of account & that the Abstracts and Balance Sheet contain a full & true account of the financial condition of the College.
rows.notes: Certification paragraph is legible though some ampersands and flourishes are present; transcribed exactly in content with slight normalization (ampersand and '&' characters). No monetary amounts on this block.

Signature line and date are present; the date 'March 8th 1901' is clear, signature reading (transcribed here as 'W.C. Tackhard F.C.O.') is moderately uncertain due to handwritten flourish—chosen reading is the best match to the script.

Grand total at bottom right in the outer £ s d columns. Clearly written as '£16002 15 7' and aligned to the outermost currency columns; used as the reference alignment for column boundaries as noted in page guidance.
